description: Write and Edit declared in allowed-tools but never referenced in any of the 5 command steps; all file operations are delegated to Bash scripts
suggested_fix: Remove Write and Edit from allowed-tools; keep Bash, Read, Glob, Grep

description: No formal ## Output section; output format guidance is embedded in step prose rather than a structured template
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output section listing the expected fields and format of the architecture summary

description: "better results" is an undefined comparator; does not specify the metric by which vineflower+dex2jar improves output
suggested_fix: Replace with "Recommend vineflower for higher-quality Java output on complex code and dex2jar for APK→JAR conversion required by fernflower"

description: "better Java output" is an undefined comparator with no metric specified
suggested_fix: Replace with "fernflower produces fewer decompilation errors on lambdas, generics, and anonymous classes"
Replace with "if the user explicitly wants to compare both decompilers for accuracy"

description: "best results" is an undefined comparator with no metric
suggested_fix: Replace with "recommended for higher-quality decompilation of complex Java patterns"

description: "better Java output" in the engine-selection table is an undefined comparator
suggested_fix: Replace with "fewer decompilation errors on lambdas/generics"

description: "broad sweep" is a vague scope quantifier; does not define which pattern categories are included
suggested_fix: Replace with "Run the API search script to scan all pattern categories (Retrofit, OkHttp, Volley, URLs, auth)"

description: pkg_install() invokes sudo apt-get/dnf/pacman unconditionally when sudo is available; package names are hardcoded so no injection risk, but sudo elevation proceeds without user confirmation
suggested_fix: Prompt the user before invoking sudo, or require an explicit --allow-sudo flag

description: add_to_profile() permanently appends export PATH and FERNFLOWER_JAR_PATH to ~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, or ~/.profile without asking the user
suggested_fix: Print the lines and ask the user to add them manually, or add a --no-profile-modify flag defaulting to off

description: install_jadx downloads a zip from GitHub releases via curl/wget with no SHA-256 or signature verification before extracting and making it executable
suggested_fix: Fetch the checksum file published alongside each GitHub release and verify with sha256sum before unzip

description: install_vineflower downloads a JAR from GitHub releases with no integrity check before setting FERNFLOWER_JAR_PATH and executing it
suggested_fix: Verify SHA-256 of downloaded JAR against the checksums published in the GitHub release

description: install_dex2jar downloads a zip from GitHub releases with no integrity check before extracting and symlinking scripts into ~/.local/bin
suggested_fix: Verify SHA-256 of downloaded zip against release checksums before extraction

description: gh_latest_tag() makes an outbound call to api.github.com to resolve the latest release tag; the response is parsed with sed/grep and used directly to construct a download URL without validation
suggested_fix: Pin to a minimum known-good version as a fallback (already done for dex2jar); consider pinning all three

description: cat prints manifest.json from a user-supplied XAPK archive directly to stdout; display-only but content is fully unsanitised
suggested_fix: Pipe through jq . to validate and pretty-print, rejecting non-JSON; or omit the cat and note the manifest path instead

description: run_grep() captures only $1 as pattern; calls run_grep -i 'regex' pass '-i' as the pattern and silently drop the regex, breaking the --auth case-insensitive search for credentials and base URLs
suggested_fix: Refactor run_grep to accept optional flags before the pattern, or add a separate run_grep_i() function that passes -i to grep
